rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports or features.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE annex blocks with proper scoping for component types.
generic_rules: Use concrete bounds or named constants in AGREE contracts for data constraints.
Ensure type consistency between AGREE expressions and AADL data types.
Avoid referencing AADL properties directly in AGREE expressions without proper scope.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference valid component ports or features.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the actual data types of the referenced features.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL timing properties directly as AGREE variables.
Do not use opaque Boolean data in numeric range comparisons.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their mapping to AGREE expressions
Implementation-level subcomponent paths in component type contracts
